title: Film Review: Beyond the Skies (云霄之上)
description: A review of Beyond the Skies (云霄之上), screened at the 2025 Beijing International Film Festival, examining its portrayal of a Red Army mission during the Chinese Civil War and its themes of sacrifice and identity.

title: Film Review: Strangers When We Meet (朝云暮雨)
description: A review of Zhang Guoli's 2024 film Strangers When We Meet, which blends absurdist comedy and tragedy in its story of a two-time convict seeking a fresh start after 27 years in prison, featuring standout performances by Fan Wei and Dongyu Zhou.

title: The languages that make my family
description: Jing-Jing Hu reflects on growing up in a multilingual Chinese household in southern Germany, where Wenzhounese, Cantonese, and German shaped her sense of family and identity.

title: Carrying Chinese-ness Across South America
description: A Chinese-American writer reflects on how traveling through South America heightened her awareness of her ethnic identity, as strangers frequently assumed her nationality and projected stereotypes about China onto her.

title: How a health scare turned a ‘boring as hell’ finance worker into a feminist comedian
description: After a health scare prompted her to leave quantitative finance, Kate Hu built a stand-up comedy career in Hong Kong, tackling identity, racism, and cultural clashes for a growing audience of nearly 20,000 followers.

title: Film Review: The Shadowless Tower (白塔之光)
description: A review of Lu Zhang's 2023 film The Shadowless Tower (白塔之光), a multigenerational drama following a melancholic Beijing food critic reconnecting with family across cities and decades.
